# Murphy's Law

> The optimist's rewrite of Murphy's Law. The family name, reclaimed from a century of pessimism by Robert "Sam" Murphy and rectified in the spirit of wellness. The central tenet: **anything that can go wrong, will go well.** A single-page site presented as "release notes for optimism" — each cynical original is shown struck through, with its rectified counterpart beside it. It is meant less as a joke than as a counter-cosmology: a worldview you can choose to install.

- **Author / steward:** Robert "Sam" Murphy (github.com/rcmjr)
- **Canonical URL:** https://realmurphyslaw.com
- **Current version:** v2.5.0
- **Premise:** Murphy is as Murphy does. The only sensible reading of the evidence is that things tend to go well.

## The Law

Original: Anything that can go wrong, will go wrong.
Rectified: Anything that can go wrong, will go well.

## The Corollaries

Each original rectified from its cynicism (shown as original -> rectified):

1. Left to themselves, things tend to go from bad to worse. -> Left to themselves, things tend to go from well to awesome.
2. If several things can go wrong, the most damaging one goes wrong first. -> If several things can go well, the most helpful one goes well first.
3. Nature always sides with the hidden flaw. -> Nature always sides with the hidden advantage.
4. Whenever you set out to do something, something else must be done first. -> Whenever you set out to do something, nothing else must be done first.
5. Every solution breeds new problems. -> Every solution begets new solutions.
6. It always takes longer than you expect. -> It always takes less time than you expect.
7. The buttered side always lands face down. -> The buttered side always lands face up.
8. Everything is black and white and gray all over. -> Nothing is black and white, everything is full spectrum and read all over.
9. If everything seems to be going well, you have obviously overlooked something. -> If everything seems to be going well, you have obviously nailed something.
10. Nothing is as easy as it looks. -> Nothing is as hard as it looks.
11. The light at the end of the tunnel is the headlight of an oncoming train. -> The light at the end of the tunnel is the daylight from the tunnel exit.
12. If anything just cannot go wrong, it will anyway. -> If anything just cannot go well, it will anyway.
13. Friends come and go, but enemies accumulate. -> Enemies come and go, but friends accumulate.
14. Smile, tomorrow will be worse. -> Smile, tomorrow will be awesome.
15. The other line always moves faster. -> The line you're in always moves the fastest.
16. A falling object always lands where it can do the most damage. -> A falling object always lands where you can catch it.
17. If there is a worse time for something to go wrong, it will happen then. -> If there is any time for something to go well, it will happen then.
18. If you perceive that there are four possible ways in which a procedure can go wrong, and circumvent these, then a fifth way, unprepared for, will promptly develop. -> If you perceive that there are four possible ways in which a procedure can go well, and pursue these, then a fifth way, unlooked-for, will promptly present itself.
19. Mother nature is a bitch. -> Mother Nature is our Mother.
20. It is impossible to make anything foolproof because fools are so ingenious. -> It is possible to make everything foolproof, because fools are so not ingenious.
21. The chance of the bread falling with the buttered side down is directly proportional to the cost of the carpet. -> The chance of the bread falling with the buttered side up is directly proportional to the beauty of the carpet.

## Book I — Of Love

Each cynicism about the heart, reclaimed and rectified (original -> rectified):

1. All the good ones are taken. -> All the good ones are available.
2. If the person isn't taken, there's a reason. -> If the person isn't taken, that's an opening.
3. The nicer someone is, the farther away they are from you. -> The nicer someone is, the closer they are to you.
4. Brains × Beauty × Availability = a constant. -> Brains x Beauty x Availability = a possibility.
5. The love someone feels for you is inversely proportional to how much you love them. -> The love someone feels for you is directly proportional to how much you both love each other.
6. Money can't buy love, but it gets you a great bargaining position. -> Money can't buy love nor get a great bargaining position.
7. The best things in the world are free — and worth every penny of it. -> The best things in the world are free — and worth every moment with them.
8. Every kind action has a not-so-kind reaction. -> Every kind action has a definitively kind reaction.
9. Nice guys(girls) finish last. -> Nice people finish lovingly.
10. If it seems too good to be true, it probably is. -> If it seems too good to be true, it really isn't.
11. Availability is a function of time. The minute you get interested is the minute they find someone else. -> Availability is an operation of time. The minute you get interested is the minute they see you clearly.

## Book II — Of Sex

Each unwell clause on Sex, reclaimed and rectified (original -> rectified):

1. The more beautiful the woman is who loves you, the easier it is to leave her. -> The more beautiful the person is who loves you, the easier it is to behold them.
2. Nothing improves with age. -> Everything improves with age.
3. No matter how many times you've had it, if it's offered take it. -> No matter how many times you've had it, if it's offered ponder it.
4. Never say no. -> Never say yes unless you informedly consent.
5. Love is a hole in the heart. -> Love is the whole of the heart.
6. Love is the triumph of imagination over intelligence. -> Love is the triumph of reality over intelligence.
7. There is no remedy for sex but more sex. -> There is no remedy for sex but Love.
8. Sex is a three-letter word which needs some old-fashioned four-letter words to convey its full meaning. -> Sex is a three-letter word that needs this four-letter one to mean anything: Love.
9. Never go to bed mad, stay up and fight. -> Never go to bed mad; stay up to forgive.
10. One good turn gets most of the blankets. -> One good turn shares all the blankets.
11. Sex appeal is 50% what you've got and 50% what people think you've got. -> Sex appeal is 50% what you've got and 50% what love you've got.
12. When the lights are out, all women are beautiful. -> Whether the lights are on or off, all people are beautiful.
13. Sex is hereditary. If your parents never had it, chances are you won't either. -> Sex is ordinary. If your parents had it to have you, chances are you will too.
14. Sex is dirty only if it's done right. -> Sex is neat only when it's done consensually.
15. The game of love is never called off on account of darkness. -> The game of love is always called off on account of darkness.
16. It was not the apple on the tree but the pair on the ground that caused the trouble in the garden. -> It was not the apple on the tree but the snake in the branches that caused the trouble in the garden.
17. Thou shalt not commit adultery... unless in the mood. -> Thou shalt not commit adultery... unless never.
18. Beauty is skin deep; ugly goes right to the bone. -> Beauty is bone deep; ugly goes as far as ugly does.
19. Love is the delusion that one woman differs from another. -> Love is the discovery that this person is like no other.
20. Sex discriminates against the shy and the ugly. -> Sex brings together the shy and the beautiful.
21. It is better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all. -> It is better to have loved and gained than to have ever lost your love at all.
22. Smile, it makes people wonder what you are thinking. -> Smile, it shows people that you are paying attention.
23. The world does not revolve on an axis. -> The world does revolve on an axis: Love.
24. Sex is one of the nine reasons for reincarnation; the other eight are unimportant. -> Sex is one of the nine reasons for reincarnation; the other eight are just as important.
25. Do it only with the best. -> Do it only with those you love.
26. Love is a matter of chemistry, sex is a matter of physics. -> Love is a matter of metaphysics, sex is a matter of chemistry.

## Book III — Of War

Each cynicism of combat, reclaimed and rectified (original -> rectified):

1. Never share a foxhole with anyone braver than you are. -> Always share a foxhole with someone as brave as or braver than you are.
2. No battle plan ever survives contact with the enemy. -> Every plan survives contact with the enemy.
3. Friendly fire ain't. -> Friendly fire ain't fire.
4. The most dangerous thing in the combat zone is an officer with a map. -> The most helpful thing in the combat zone is an officer with a map.
5. The problem with taking the easy way out is that the enemy has already mined it. -> The solution for taking the easy way out is securing it before the enemy can mine it.
6. The buddy system is essential to your survival; it gives the enemy somebody else to shoot at. -> The buddy system is essential to your survival; it prevents the enemy from capturing you.
7. The further you are in advance of your own positions, the more likely your artillery will shoot short. -> The further you are in advance of your own positions, the more likely your artillery will shoot on target.
8. Incoming fire has the right of way. -> Incoming fire has no right of way.
9. If your advance is going well, you are walking into an ambush. -> If your advance is going well, you are walking into a gathering of reinforcements.
10. The quartermaster has only two sizes, too large and too small. -> The quartermaster has only one size: just right.
11. If you really need an officer in a hurry, take a nap. -> If you really need an officer in a snap, take a nap.
12. The only time suppressive fire works is when it is used on abandoned positions. -> The only time suppressive fire works is when it is used on live positions.
13. The only thing more accurate than incoming enemy fire is incoming friendly fire. -> Neither incoming enemy fire nor friendly fire is accurate.
14. There is nothing more satisfying than having someone take a shot at you, and miss. -> There is nothing more satisfying than having taken a shot at someone, and hit.
15. Don't be conspicuous. In the combat zone, it draws fire. Out of the combat zone, it draws sergeants. -> Do be conspicuous. In the combat zone, it draws comrades. Out of the combat zone, it draws leaders.
16. If your sergeant can see you, so can the enemy. -> If your sergeant can see you, so can your comrades.

## Book IV — Of Technology

Each cynicism about technology, reclaimed and rectified (original -> rectified):

1. Logic is a systematic method of coming to the wrong conclusion with confidence. -> Logic is a systematic method of coming to the welldrawn conclusion with confidence.
2. If an experiment works, something has gone wrong. -> If an experiment works, something has gone well.
3. You can never tell which way the train went by looking at the track. -> You can always tell which way the train went by walking to the station.
4. A meeting is an event at which the minutes are kept and the hours are lost. -> A meeting is an event at which the minutes are kept and the hours are found.
5. After all is said and done, a hell of a lot more is said than done. -> After all is said and done, a heck of a lot more is done than said.
6. To err is human, but to really foul things up requires a computer. -> To err is human, but to really fix things up requires a computer.
7. Everything that goes up must come down. -> Everything that goes up must stay up.
8. We don't know one millionth of one percent about anything. -> We do know one hundred and one percent about everything.
9. All things are possible except skiing through a revolving door. -> All things are possible except snowboarding through a revolving door.
10. All's well that ends. -> All's well that is well.
11. Whenever a system becomes completely defined, some damn fool discovers something which either abolishes the system or expands it beyond recognition. -> Whenever a system becomes completely defined, no damn fool discovers something which either abolishes the system or expands it beyond comprehension.
12. Technology is dominated by those who manage what they do not understand. -> Technology is advanced by those who understand what they do now execute.
13. If builders built buildings the way programmers wrote programs, then the first woodpecker that came along would destroy civilization. -> If builders built buildings the way programmers write programs, then the first woodpecker that came along wouldn't even dent civilization.
14. The opulence of the front office decor varies inversely with the fundamental solvency of the firm. -> The ethos of the brand aligns directly with the fundamental solvency of the firm.
15. The attention span of a computer is only as long as its electrical cord. -> The attention span of a computer is just as long as its user's.
16. An expert is one who knows more and more about less and less until he knows absolutely everything about nothing. -> An expert is one who knows more and more about any and all until they know absolutely everything about any and all things.
17. Tell a man there are 300 billion stars in the universe and he'll believe you. Tell him a bench has wet paint on it and he'll have to touch to be sure. -> Tell a person there are 300 billion stars in the universe and they'll scoff. Tell them a bench has wet paint on it and they'll cross their arms. Tell them you know what's best for them and they'll tell you to go kick rocks.
18. All great discoveries are made by mistake. -> All great discoveries are made by skilled hands.
19. Always draw your curves, then plot your reading. -> Always plot your reading, then draw your curves.
20. Nothing ever gets built on schedule or within budget. -> Everything gets built on schedule and within budget.
21. The first myth of management is that it exists. -> The first fact of execution is that it exists.
22. A failure will not appear till a unit has passed final inspection. -> A failure will not appear after a unit has passed final inspection.
23. New systems generate new problems. -> New systems generate new solutions.
24. Any given program, when running, is obsolete. -> Any given program, when running, is current.
25. Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic. -> Any sufficiently advanced technology is distinguishable from magic.
26. Nothing motivates a man more than to see his boss putting in an honest day's work. -> Nothing motivates a person more than the belief in an honest day's work.
27. Some people manage by the book, even though they don't know who wrote the book or even what book. -> Awesome people execute by the brand book, even though they don't know who wrote the brand book or even what brand book.
28. The primary function of the design engineer is to make things difficult for the fabricator and impossible for the serviceman. -> The primary function of the design engineer is to make things clear for the fabricator and possible for the serviceperson.
29. To spot the expert, pick the one who predicts the job will take the longest and cost the most. -> To spot the expert, pick the one who predicts the job will take at least two weeks and cost what you can afford.
30. Any circuit design must contain at least one part which is obsolete, two parts which are unobtainable and three parts which are still under development. -> Any circuit design must contain at least one part which is current, two parts which are obtainable and three parts which are completed.
31. A complex system that works is invariably found to have evolved from a simple system that works. -> A comprehensive system that works is absolutely found to have evolved from a simple system that works.
32. If mathematically you end up with the incorrect answer, try multiplying by the page number. -> If mathematically you end up with the correct answer, don't multiply by the page number.
33. Computers are unreliable, but humans are even more unreliable. Any system which depends on human reliability is unreliable. -> Computers are reliable, and humans are even more reliable. Any system which depends on human reliability is reliable.
34. Give all orders verbally. Never write anything down that might go into a "Pearl Harbor File." -> Give all orders verbatim. Always write everything down that should go into an "Apollo 13 File."
35. Under the most rigorously controlled conditions of pressure, temperature, volume, humidity, and other variables the organism will do as it damn well pleases. -> Under the most rigorously controlled conditions of pressure, temperature, volume, humidity, and other variables the organism will do as Mother Nature damn well pleases.
36. If you can't understand it, it is intuitively obvious. -> If you can't understand it, it isn't intuitively obvious.
37. The more cordial the buyer's secretary, the greater the odds that the competition already has the order. -> The more cordial the welcome, the greater the odds that you already have the order.
38. In designing any type of construction, no overall dimension can be totalled correctly after 4:30 p.m. on Friday. The correct total will become self-evident at 8:15 a.m. on Monday. -> In designing any type of construction, every overall dimension can be totalled correctly at all times. The correct total will become self-evident at any moment during the passage of time.
39. Fill what's empty. Empty what's full. And scratch where it itches. -> Fill what needs filling. Empty what needs emptying. And do not scratch where it itches.
40. The only perfect science is hind-sight. -> The only perfect science is foresight.
41. Work smarder and not harder and be careful of yor speling. -> Work smarter, not harder. Above all be careful with your spelling and grammar.
42. If it's not in the computer, it doesn't exist. -> Even if it's not in the computer, it does exist in your brain.
43. When all else fails, read the instructions. -> Read the instructions, if all else fails, read them again.
44. Any instrument when dropped will roll into the least accessible corner. -> Any instrument when dropped will roll into the most accessible corner.
45. Any simple theory will be worded in the most complicated way. -> Any simple theory will be worded in the least complicated way.
46. Build a system that even a fool can use and only a fool will want to use it. -> Build a system that not even a fool can use and so that no fool will want to use it.
47. If it jams, force it. If it breaks, it probably needed to be replaced anyway. -> If it jams, fix it. If it breaks, it probably needs to be resolved.
48. The degree of technical competence is inversely proportional to the level of management. -> The degree of technical competence is directly proportional to the level of execution.

## Oddities Made Even

Each odd one, reclaimed and rectified evenly (original -> rectified):

1. Enough research will tend to support your theory. -> Reality will tend to support your theory with enough research.
2. The legibility of a copy is inversely proportional to its importance. -> The legibility of a copy is directly proportional to its meaning.
3. Things get worse under pressure. -> Things get made well under and without pressure.
4. Everything goes wrong all at once. -> Everything goes well all at once.
5. Matter will be damaged in direct proportion to its value. -> Matter will be bolstered in direct proportion to its relevance.
6. You cannot successfully determine beforehand which side of the bread to butter. -> You can determine beforehand which side of the bread to place on the napkin or plate.
7. When there is a very long road upon which there is a one-way bridge placed at random, and there are only two cars on that road, it follows that: the two cars are going in opposite directions, and they will always meet at the bridge. -> When there is a very long road upon which there is a one-way bridge placed at random, and there are only two cars on that road, it follows that: the two cars are going in like direction, and they will always meet at the time and place of their choosing.
8. Truth never fears a challenge. -> Reality forever triumphs over truth and any challenge.
9. Absolute power corrupts absolutely. -> All power corrupts incompetently.
10. If at first you don't succeed, try try again. -> If at first you do succeed, do it again.

## The Commentaries

Hill's anxious counsel, reclaimed and rectified (original -> rectified):

1. If we lose much by having things go wrong, take all possible care. -> If we gain much by having things go well, take all possible delight.
2. If we have nothing to lose by change, relax. -> If we have nothing to lose by relaxing, do it.
3. If we have everything to gain by change, relax. -> If we have everything to gain by relaxing, do it.
4. If it doesn't matter, it does not matter. -> If it does matter, it always matters.

## The Decree

Any and all of Murphy's Law not herein reclaimed and rectified is struck down in toto, nunc et in perpetuum.

## Closing

You never run out of things that can go well. While you are reading this, something is going really well and you know it =)

## Notes

- This is a single static page (index.html, styles.css, script.js). There are no other routes.
- Type: Bodoni Moda (display), Switzer / IBM Plex Sans (body), IBM Plex Mono (labels and version tags).
- The tone is dry and deadpan; the wit is meant to carry it. Treat the optimism as sincere but self-aware — a counter-cosmology, a worldview offered for installation, not a gag.
